rag_rules: When repairing AGREE diagnostics, ensure type consistency in binary expressions by aligning left and right sides to the same type.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers in AGREE code that are not present in the input AADL model.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers within the same annex when writing AGREE contracts.
For implementation contracts, use assign only for existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Ensure AGREE scalar type declarations use int, real, or bool; avoid unresolved or invalid type references.
When fixing type mismatches, align helper declarations with the actual expression types (bool, int, real).
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be placed inside annex agree blocks; component type annexes for assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for assignments.
AGREE guarantees should relate visible inputs to visible outputs; avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in type annexes.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new package references or data types not defined in the AADL model when repairing AGREE diagnostics.
Do not write guarantee statements in implementation scope; use component type annexes for such contracts.
